title: Gymnogramma scandens
intro: Gymnogramma scandens is a type of fern, specifically a species of the family Polypodiaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Gymnogramma scandens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gymnogramma scandens is often prepared as a decoction or a tea, and is also used in the production of infusions and tinctures for its medicinal and therapeutic applications.
taxonomy: Gymnogramma scandens is classified within the family Compositae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as asterids. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics, including its inflorescence structure and seed morphology. The family Compositae is further divided into several subfamilies, with Gymnogramma scandens being a part of the subfamily Mutisioideae. The subfamily Mutisioideae is a distinct group of plants that are primarily native to the southern hemisphere. Gymnogramma scandens is likely to be closely related to other species within this subfamily due to its shared characteristics and evolutionary history.

More research is needed to confirm the plant's full range of health benefits and potential uses.
